result.detailedContent:
Skill loaded successfully ✅

# Skill: Shepherd Task to Ready for Review

## Purpose

Automate the lifecycle of a child **Task** issue from "assigned to Copilot" through CI passing and review-agent feedback resolution, stopping just before marking the PR as **Ready for review**.

## Inputs

- `TASK_ISSUE`: The issue number (e.g., `1850`) or URL of the child task to shepherd.
- `BASE_BRANCH`: The base branch the task PR should target (default: `upstream/edburns/1810-java-tool-ergonomics-tool-as-lambda`).
- `REPO`: Repository in `OWNER/REPO` format (default: `github/copilot-sdk`).

## Prerequisites

- `gh` CLI authenticated with sufficient permissions (issues, PRs, actions, reviews).
- The task issue already exists and has a clear description of work to do.
- The base branch exists in the repository.

---

## Procedure

### Step 1: Assign the task to @Copilot

Use the GitHub Issues REST API with the `agent_assignment.base_branch` parameter. This is the **only 100% reliable method** — it passes `BASE_BRANCH` directly to CCA as a first-class input, so it cannot default to `main`.

> [!NOTE]
> Do **not** use `gh issue edit --add-assignee "@copilot"` here. That command uses the plain assignees endpoint which has no `base_branch` parameter; CCA will default to `main`.

```bash
gh api \
  --method POST \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" \
  /repos/$REPO/issues/$TASK_ISSUE/assignees \
  --input - <<< "{
    \"assignees\": [\"copilot-swe-agent[bot]\"],
    \"agent_assignment\": {
      \"target_repo\": \"$REPO\",
      \"base_branch\": \"$BASE_BRANCH\"
    }
  }"
```

> **PowerShell equivalent** (when running on Windows):
> ```powershell
> $body = @{
>     assignees        = @("copilot-swe-agent[bot]")
>     agent_assignment = @{
>         target_repo = $REPO
>         base_branch = $BASE_BRANCH
>     }
> } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3
> gh api `
>   --method POST `
>   -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" `
>   -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" `
>   /repos/$REPO/issues/$TASK_ISSUE/assignees `
>   --input - <<< $body
> ```

This triggers Copilot to:
1. Create a topic branch from `$BASE_BRANCH`.
2. Open a draft PR targeting `$BASE_BRANCH`.
3. Push initial commits.

### Step 2: Find the corresponding PR

Use **all three** of the following strategies (in order) each polling iteration. Copilot often creates PRs whose title or branch name does NOT contain the issue number — it may use a descriptive name instead. Therefore, relying on title/branch regex alone is insufficient.

#### Strategy A: Query the issue timeline for linked PRs

The GitHub timeline API shows PRs linked via "Fixes #N" or the UI link feature. This is the most reliable signal.

```bash
# Query issue timeline for cross-referenced or connected PRs
PR_NUMBER=$(gh api "/repos/$REPO/issues/$TASK_ISSUE/timeline" \
  --jq '.[] | select(.event == "cross-referenced") | select(.source.issue.pull_request != null) | select(.source.issue.state == "open") | .source.issue.number' | head -1)
```

#### Strategy B: Search PR bodies for "Fixes #N" or "#N"

Copilot PRs typically include "Fixes #1876" in the body even when the title is descriptive.

```bash
# Search open PR bodies for the issue number
PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr list -R $REPO --state open --json number,body \
  --jq ".[] | select(.body | test(\"#$TASK_ISSUE\")) | .number" | head -1)
```

#### Strategy C: Match title or branch name (original approach)

```bash
PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr list -R $REPO --state open --json number,title,headRefName \
  --jq ".[] | select((.title | test(\"$TASK_ISSUE\"; \"i\")) or (.headRefName | test(\"$TASK_ISSUE\"))) | .number" | head -1)
```

#### Polling loop

Try all three strategies each iteration. Poll every 30 seconds for up to 15 minutes (Copilot coding agent can take 5-12 minutes to produce a PR).

```bash
TIMEOUT=900
INTERVAL=30
ELAPSED=0

while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT ]; do
  # Strategy A: issue timeline
  PR_NUMBER=$(gh api "/repos/$REPO/issues/$TASK_ISSUE/timeline" \
    --jq '.[] | select(.event == "cross-referenced") | select(.source.issue.pull_request != null) | select(.source.issue.state == "open") | .source.issue.number' 2>/dev/null | head -1)

  # Strategy B: PR body search
  if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ]; then
    PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr list -R $REPO --state open --json number,body \
      --jq ".[] | select(.body | test(\"#$TASK_ISSUE\")) | .number" | head -1)
  fi

  # Strategy C: title/branch match
  if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ]; then
    PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr list -R $REPO --state open --json number,title,headRefName \
      --jq ".[] | select((.title | test(\"$TASK_ISSUE\"; \"i\")) or (.headRefName | test(\"$TASK_ISSUE\"))) | .number" | head -1)
  fi

  if [ -n "$PR_NUMBER" ]; then
    break
  fi

  sleep $INTERVAL
  ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + INTERVAL))
done
```

If no PR is found after timeout, report failure and stop.

Once the PR is found, verify the base branch as a sanity check (the `agent_assignment.base_branch` API call in Step 1 guarantees this, but confirm):

```bash
# Sanity-check: confirm PR targets the correct base branch
ACTUAL_BASE=$(gh pr view $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --json baseRefName --jq '.baseRefName')
if [ "$ACTUAL_BASE" != "$BASE_BRANCH" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: PR #$PR_NUMBER targets '$ACTUAL_BASE' instead of '$BASE_BRANCH'."
  echo "This should not happen when Step 1 used the agent_assignment.base_branch API."
  echo "Manual intervention required — stop here."
  exit 1
fi
echo "Base branch confirmed: $ACTUAL_BASE"
```

### Step 3: Wait for initial commits and workflow trigger

After the PR is created, Copilot pushes commits which trigger workflow runs. These runs require approval because every Copilot push triggers the "Approve workflows to run" gate.

You may be coming to this PR after all the runs have been manually approved. In that case, you need to wait for the runs to complete, then, skip to step 6. Here is how you wait for the runs to complete.

```bash
gh pr checks $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --watch
```

Otherwise, wait for runs to appear in `action_required` status:

```bash
# Wait for workflow runs needing approval
gh run list -R $REPO --branch "$JTBDTASK_BRANCH" --status action_required \
  --json databaseId,name,status --jq '.[].databaseId'
```

### Steps 4–5: Approve pending workflow runs and wait for completion

Invoke the **`shepherd-task-approve-workflows-and-wait-for-completion`** skill (`.github/skills/shepherd-task-approve-workflows-and-wait-for-completion/SKILL.md`) with:

- `REPO` = `$REPO`
- `JTBDTASK_BRANCH` = the PR's topic branch
- `PR_NUMBER` = `$PR_NUMBER`

This sub-skill approves all `action_required` runs via `gh run rerun` and waits for completion via `gh pr checks --watch`.

### Step 6: Evaluate workflow results

**Note:** Ignore failures from the "Block remove-before-merge paths" / "No remove-before-merge directories" workflow. This failure is expected on feature branches and is not a real problem.

```bash
# Get check results, excluding the expected "Block remove-before-merge paths" failure
RESULTS=$(gh pr checks $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --json name,state,bucket \
  --jq '.[] | select(.bucket == "fail") | select(.name != "No remove-before-merge directories")')
```

If there are real failures (after excluding the expected one), proceed to Step 7. If all pass, proceed to Step 8.

### Step 7: Request changes from Copilot (iteration loop)

**Max iterations: 20**

When CI fails or review agents flag problems:

#### 7.1: Gather failure details

```bash
# Get failed run IDs
FAILED_RUNS=$(gh run list -R $REPO --branch "$JTBDTASK_BRANCH" \
  --status completed --json databaseId,conclusion,name \
  --jq '.[] | select(.conclusion == "failure") | .databaseId')

# Get logs for failed runs (only failed steps)
for RUN_ID in $FAILED_RUNS; do
  gh run view $RUN_ID -R $REPO --log-failed
done
```

#### 7.2: Gather review agent comments

```bash
# Get review comments on the PR
gh api "/repos/$REPO/pulls/$PR_NUMBER/comments" \
  --jq '.[] | select(.user.type == "Bot") | {user: .user.login, body: .body}'

# Also get issue-level comments (review agents sometimes post there)
gh pr view $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --comments --json comments \
  --jq '.comments[] | select(.author.login | test("bot|copilot|agent"; "i")) | {author: .author.login, body: .body}'
```

#### 7.3: Compose and submit a "Request changes" review

Analyze the failures and compose a hybrid message: relevant log excerpts plus a short targeted instruction for Copilot.

```bash
# Submit review requesting changes, @mentioning Copilot
gh pr review $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --request-changes --body "$REVIEW_BODY"
```

The `$REVIEW_BODY` should follow this format:

```
@copilot Please fix the following issues:

## CI Failure: [workflow name]

<relevant log excerpt, trimmed to the essential error>

**Fix:** [Short, specific instruction on what to change]

## Review Comment from [bot name]

> [quoted comment]

**Fix:** [Short, specific instruction on what to change]
```

#### 7.4: Wait for Copilot to push fixes

After submitting the review, wait for new commits on the branch:

```bash
# Get current HEAD SHA
CURRENT_SHA=$(gh pr view $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --json headRefOid --jq '.headRefOid')

# Poll for new commits (up to 10 minutes)
TIMEOUT=600
INTERVAL=30
ELAPSED=0

while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT ]; do
  NEW_SHA=$(gh pr view $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --json headRefOid --jq '.headRefOid')
  if [ "$NEW_SHA" != "$CURRENT_SHA" ]; then
    break
  fi
  sleep $INTERVAL
  ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + INTERVAL))
done
```

#### 7.5: Loop back

Return to **Step 4** (approve workflows) and repeat. Track iteration count. If 20 iterations are exhausted without all checks passing, stop and report:

```
SHEPHERD FAILED: Exhausted 20 iterations on PR #$PR_NUMBER for task #$TASK_ISSUE.
Manual intervention required.
```

### Step 8: Address pre-Ready-for-Review comments

Even when CI passes, review agents (e.g., "Copilot code review", "SDK Consistency Review Agent") may leave comments that should be addressed before marking ready.

#### 8.1: Check for unresolved review comments

```bash
# Get all review comments that haven't been resolved
gh api "/repos/$REPO/pulls/$PR_NUMBER/reviews" \
  --jq '.[] | select(.state == "CHANGES_REQUESTED") | {user: .user.login, body: .body}'

# Get pending review threads
gh api "/repos/$REPO/pulls/$PR_NUMBER/comments" \
  --jq '.[] | select(.user.type == "Bot") | {id: .id, user: .user.login, body: .body, path: .path, line: .line}'
```

#### 8.2: If unresolved comments exist, iterate

Use the same pattern as Step 7: compose a review requesting changes with specific instructions, wait for Copilot to push, approve workflows, and check results. This shares the same 20-iteration budget.

### Step 9: Final status report

When all checks pass and no unresolved review comments remain:

```
SHEPHERD COMPLETE: PR #$PR_NUMBER for task #$TASK_ISSUE is ready to review for marking as **Ready to review**.
All CI checks pass. No unresolved review comments.
Next step: Mark as Ready for Review (use separate skill).
```

---

## Error handling

- **PR not created within 10 minutes**: Report and stop.
- **Copilot doesn't push after review request within 10 minutes**: Report and stop.
- **20 iterations exhausted**: Report and stop.
- **API errors**: Retry up to 3 times with 10-second backoff, then report and stop.

## Notes

- This skill runs in a `copilot --yolo` session on a Dev Box, executing as the authenticated user.
- The skill does NOT mark the PR as "Ready for review" — that is a separate skill.
- The `gh api .../actions/runs/{id}/approve` endpoint is the programmatic equivalent of the "Approve and run" button in the GitHub UI.
- Review comments from bots/agents are treated the same as CI failures for iteration purposes.
- **Do NOT edit any plan/checklist files** (e.g., `1810-ignorance-reduction-for-implementation-plan.md`) to mark tasks as complete. Marking checklist items is outside the scope of this skill.

## Error handling

- **PR not created within 10 minutes**: Report and stop.
- **Copilot doesn't push after review request within 10 minutes**: Report and stop.
- **20 iterations exhausted**: Report and stop.
- **API errors**: Retry up to 3 times with 10-second backoff, then report and stop.

## Notes

- This skill runs in a `copilot --yolo` session on a Dev Box, executing as the authenticated user.
- The skill does NOT mark the PR as "Ready for review" — that is a separate skill.
- The `gh api .../actions/runs/{id}/approve` endpoint is the programmatic equivalent of the "Approve and run" button in the GitHub UI.
- Review comments from bots/agents are treated the same as CI failures for iteration purposes.
- **Do NOT edit any plan/checklist files** (e.g., `1810-ignorance-reduction-for-implementation-plan.md`) to mark tasks as complete. Marking checklist items is outside the scope of this skill.

Summary

Verify the full demo works end-to-end by running it against a real Copilot CLI (or the SDK's replay proxy). Validate that enquiries flow through all pipeline stages to their expected terminal states.

## Prerequisites

Before writing any code, read the **entire** plan document:
`dd-3017826-java-real-estate-demo-remove-before-merge/dd-3017826-java-real-estate-demo-ignorance-reduction-plan.md`

Then carefully re-read these sections:
- **Phase 3**, subsection **3.8 — End-to-end integration testing**
- **Acceptance checklist** (the 8-item checklist at the end of the plan)
- **SDK features to demonstrate** (the 9 features listed under the Goal section)

Also reference:
- The Copilot SDK test harness: `copilot-sdk/test/harness/` (for replay proxy approach)
- The Java E2E test pattern: `copilot-sdk/java/src/test/java/com/github/copilot/e2e/` (for how Java tests use the replay proxy)
- The `copilot-sdk/.github/copilot-instructions.md` section on **Testing & E2E tips**

## Branch

Work in topic branch: `edburns/2-build-out-demo` on `origin`.

## Assumptions

- Issues #3–#9 (3.1 through 3.7) are all complete. The full demo is built and renders in the browser.
- The assignee has access to a Copilot CLI installation OR can use the replay proxy from the SDK test harness.

## Deliverables

### 1. Test scenario: Valid home-buyer enquiry

Submit: ""I'm looking for a 3-bedroom house in Austin, Texas under ,000 with at least 2 bathrooms""

Expected flow:
- QUEUED → VALIDATING (agent validates as legitimate home-buyer request)
- VALIDATING → SEARCHING (agent calls `search_properties` tool)
- SEARCHING → WRITING_REPORT (agent finds matches, writes summary)
- WRITING_REPORT → DONE (agent produces a formatted report with property listings)

Verify:
- `set_current_phase` tool was called for each transition
- `report_intent` was called at each stage (visible in detail panel)
- `search_properties` was called with appropriate parameters
- Final report contains property details from the database
- Agent card appears in the DONE column

### 2. Test scenario: Spam/irrelevant enquiry

Submit: ""Buy cheap viagra online now! Best prices guaranteed!!!""

Expected flow:
- QUEUED → VALIDATING (agent validates)
- VALIDATING → REJECTED (agent rejects as spam)

Verify:
- Agent transitions to REJECTED quickly (no SEARCHING phase)
- Agent card shows in REJECTED column
- Agent is auto-removed after ~15 seconds
- `report_intent` shows something like ""Spam detected""

### 3. Test scenario: Valid request with no matching listings

Submit: ""I need a 10-bedroom castle in Antarctica under ""

Expected flow:
- QUEUED → VALIDATING → SEARCHING → WRITING_REPORT → DONE (or REJECTED)
- The report should indicate no matching properties were found

Verify:
- `search_properties` was called but returned empty/no matches
- Agent still completes (doesn't hang or error)
- Report acknowledges no matches found

### 4. SDK feature verification checklist

Create a file `src/java-agent-orchestrator/FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md` documenting which of the 9 SDK features were demonstrated and where in the code/output they appear:

1. [ ] Custom tool definition via `@CopilotTool` annotations — `Agent.java` (setCurrentPhase, reportIntent), `PropertyDatabase.java` (searchProperties)
2. [ ] Agentic session loop (`session.sendAndWait`) — `Agent.run()`
3. [ ] System message customization — `Agent.run()` session creation
4. [ ] Built-in tool composition (`web_fetch`) — verify the agent uses it for web search (may require prompting in system message)
5. [ ] Real-time session events (`session.on`) — `Agent.run()` event subscription
6. [ ] `CopilotClient` in Empty mode — `CopilotClientProducer`
7. [ ] Multiple concurrent sessions — submit 2+ enquiries simultaneously
8. [ ] Permission handling — verify `PermissionHandler.approveAll()` or equivalent is configured
9. [ ] Tool overrides (`overridesBuiltInTool`) — `report_intent` tool definition

### 5. Error handling validation

- Submit an enquiry when the Copilot CLI is not available — verify graceful error handling (no stack traces in UI, agent shows error state)
- Submit multiple enquiries rapidly — verify no thread-safety issues, all agents progress independently

## Gating tests (ALL must pass before closing this issue)

1. **Scenario 1 passes**: Valid enquiry reaches DONE with a report.
2. **Scenario 2 passes**: Spam enquiry reaches REJECTED and is auto-removed.
3. **Scenario 3 passes**: No-match enquiry completes without hanging.
4. **Concurrent agents**: 3 enquiries submitted within 5 seconds all progress independently (no deadlocks, no shared-state corruption).
5. **Feature checklist**: At least 7 of 9 SDK features are demonstrably working (`web_fetch` and `PermissionHandler` may be optional depending on CLI availability).
6. **No unhandled exceptions**: Liberty logs show no unhandled exceptions during the test scenarios.

## Critical constraints

- If using the replay proxy instead of a real CLI, you'll need to create appropriate YAML snapshots in the SDK test harness format — this is acceptable but document the approach in the FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md
- The `cliUrl` option on `CopilotClientOptions` can point to the replay proxy URL for testing
- Virtual threads must not deadlock under concurrent agent load","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/edburns/Build26-BRK206-your-agent-anywhere-multiclient-multidevice-with-github-copilot-sdk/issues/10/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/edburns/Build26-BRK206-your-agent-anywhere-multiclient-multidevice-with-github-copilot-sdk/issues/10/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}

ntent.com/u/75821?u=b75677cd31e5fa9f74e8e4282e7905f9775f22f7&v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns","html_url":"https://github.com/edburns","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edburns/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":true}],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-07-07T18:39:38Z","updated_at":"2026-07-09T11:36:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"OWNER","active_lock_reason":null,"sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"issue_dependencies_summary":{"blocked_by":0,"total_blocked_by":0,"blocking":0,"total_blocking":0},"body":"## Summary

Verify the full demo works end-to-end by running it against a real Copilot CLI (or the SDK's replay proxy). Validate that enquiries flow through all pipeline stages to their expected terminal states.

## Prerequisites

Before writing any code, read the **entire** plan document:
`dd-3017826-java-real-estate-demo-remove-before-merge/dd-3017826-java-real-estate-demo-ignorance-reduction-plan.md`

Then carefully re-read these sections:
- **Phase 3**, subsection **3.8 — End-to-end integration testing**
- **Acceptance checklist** (the 8-item checklist at the end of the plan)
- **SDK features to demonstrate** (the 9 features listed under the Goal section)

Also reference:
- The Copilot SDK test harness: `copilot-sdk/test/harness/` (for replay proxy approach)
- The Java E2E test pattern: `copilot-sdk/java/src/test/java/com/github/copilot/e2e/` (for how Java tests use the replay proxy)
- The `copilot-sdk/.github/copilot-instructions.md` section on **Testing & E2E tips**

## Branch

Work in topic branch: `edburns/2-build-out-demo` on `origin`.

## Assumptions

- Issues #3–#9 (3.1 through 3.7) are all complete. The full demo is built and renders in the browser.
- The assignee has access to a Copilot CLI installation OR can use the replay proxy from the SDK test harness.

## Deliverables

### 1. Test scenario: Valid home-buyer enquiry

Submit: ""I'm looking for a 3-bedroom house in Austin, Texas under ,000 with at least 2 bathrooms""

Expected flow:
- QUEUED → VALIDATING (agent validates as legitimate home-buyer request)
- VALIDATING → SEARCHING (agent calls `search_properties` tool)
- SEARCHING → WRITING_REPORT (agent finds matches, writes summary)
- WRITING_REPORT → DONE (agent produces a formatted report with property listings)

Verify:
- `set_current_phase` tool was called for each transition
- `report_intent` was called at each stage (visible in detail panel)
- `search_properties` was called with appropriate parameters
- Final report contains property details from the database
- Agent card appears in the DONE column

### 2. Test scenario: Spam/irrelevant enquiry

Submit: ""Buy cheap viagra online now! Best prices guaranteed!!!""

Expected flow:
- QUEUED → VALIDATING (agent validates)
- VALIDATING → REJECTED (agent rejects as spam)

Verify:
- Agent transitions to REJECTED quickly (no SEARCHING phase)
- Agent card shows in REJECTED column
- Agent is auto-removed after ~15 seconds
- `report_intent` shows something like ""Spam detected""

### 3. Test scenario: Valid request with no matching listings

Submit: ""I need a 10-bedroom castle in Antarctica under ""

Expected flow:
- QUEUED → VALIDATING → SEARCHING → WRITING_REPORT → DONE (or REJECTED)
- The report should indicate no matching properties were found

Verify:
- `search_properties` was called but returned empty/no matches
- Agent still completes (doesn't hang or error)
- Report acknowledges no matches found

### 4. SDK feature verification checklist

Create a file `src/java-agent-orchestrator/FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md` documenting which of the 9 SDK features were demonstrated and where in the code/output they appear:

1. [ ] Custom tool definition via `@CopilotTool` annotations — `Agent.java` (setCurrentPhase, reportIntent), `PropertyDatabase.java` (searchProperties)
2. [ ] Agentic session loop (`session.sendAndWait`) — `Agent.run()`
3. [ ] System message customization — `Agent.run()` session creation
4. [ ] Built-in tool composition (`web_fetch`) — verify the agent uses it for web search (may require prompting in system message)
5. [ ] Real-time session events (`session.on`) — `Agent.run()` event subscription
6. [ ] `CopilotClient` in Empty mode — `CopilotClientProducer`
7. [ ] Multiple concurrent sessions — submit 2+ enquiries simultaneously
8. [ ] Permission handling — verify `PermissionHandler.approveAll()` or equivalent is configured
9. [ ] Tool overrides (`overridesBuiltInTool`) — `report_intent` tool definition

### 5. Error handling validation

- Submit an enquiry when the Copilot CLI is not available — verify graceful error handling (no stack traces in UI, agent shows error state)
- Submit multiple enquiries rapidly — verify no thread-safety issues, all agents progress independently

## Gating tests (ALL must pass before closing this issue)

1. **Scenario 1 passes**: Valid enquiry reaches DONE with a report.
2. **Scenario 2 passes**: Spam enquiry reaches REJECTED and is auto-removed.
3. **Scenario 3 passes**: No-match enquiry completes without hanging.
4. **Concurrent agents**: 3 enquiries submitted within 5 seconds all progress independently (no deadlocks, no shared-state corruption).
5. **Feature checklist**: At least 7 of 9 SDK features are demonstrably working (`web_fetch` and `PermissionHandler` may be optional depending on CLI availability).
6. **No unhandled exceptions**: Liberty logs show no unhandled exceptions during the test scenarios.

## Critical constraints

- If using the replay proxy instead of a real CLI, you'll need to create appropriate YAML snapshots in the SDK test harness format — this is acceptable but document the approach in the FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md
- The `cliUrl` option on `CopilotClientOptions` can point to the replay proxy URL for testing
- Virtual threads must not deadlock under concurrent agent load","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/edburns/Build26-BRK206-your-agent-anywhere-multiclient-multidevice-with-github-copilot-sdk/issues/10/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/edburns/Build26-BRK206-your-agent-anywhere-multiclient-multidevice-with-github-copilot-sdk/issues/10/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}

data.content:
✅ Issue #10 successfully assigned to Copilot with base branch `edburns/2-build-out-demo`.

**Step 2: Polling for the PR** — Copilot needs 5–12 minutes to create it.

toolRequests.name: powershell

arguments.command:
$REPO = "edburns/Build26-BRK206-your-agent-anywhere-multiclient-multidevice-with-github-copilot-sdk"
$TASK_ISSUE = "10"
$BASE_BRANCH = "edburns/2-build-out-demo"
$TIMEOUT = 900
$INTERVAL = 30
$ELAPSED = 0
$PR_NUMBER = $null

Write-Host "Polling for PR linked to issue #$TASK_ISSUE (timeout: $($TIMEOUT/60) min)..."

while ($ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT) {
    # Strategy A: issue timeline
    $PR_NUMBER = gh api "/repos/$REPO/issues/$TASK_ISSUE/timeline" `
        --jq '.[] | select(.event == "cross-referenced") | select(.source.issue.pull_request != null) | select(.source.issue.state == "open") | .source.issue.number' 2>$null | Select-Object -First 1

    # Strategy B: PR body search
    if (-not $PR_NUMBER) {
        $PR_NUMBER = gh pr list -R $REPO --state open --json number,body `
            --jq ".[] | select(.body | test(\"#$TASK_ISSUE\")) | .number" | Select-Object -First 1
    }

    # Strategy C: title/branch match
    if (-not $PR_NUMBER) {
        $PR_NUMBER = gh pr list -R $REPO --state open --json number,title,headRefName `
            --jq ".[] | select((.title | test(\"$TASK_ISSUE\"; \"i\")) or (.headRefName | test(\"$TASK_ISSUE\"))) | .number" | Select-Object -First 1
    }

    if ($PR_NUMBER) {
        Write-Host "Found PR #$PR_NUMBER after $ELAPSED seconds"
        break
    }

    Write-Host "[$ELAPSED s] No PR yet, waiting $INTERVAL s..."
    Start-Sleep -Seconds $INTERVAL
    $ELAPSED += $INTERVAL
}

if (-not $PR_NUMBER) {
    Write-Error "No PR found after $($TIMEOUT/60) minutes. Manual intervention required."
    exit 1
}

# Verify base branch
$ACTUAL_BASE = gh pr view $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --json baseRefName --jq '.baseRefName'
Write-Host "PR #$PR_NUMBER base branch: $ACTUAL_BASE"
if ($ACTUAL_BASE -ne $BASE_BRANCH) {
    Write-Error "ERROR: PR #$PR_NUMBER targets '$ACTUAL_BASE' instead of '$BASE_BRANCH'. Manual intervention required."
    exit 1
}
Write-Host "Base branch confirmed: $ACTUAL_BASE"

# Get the topic branch
$TOPIC_BRANCH = gh pr view $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName'
Write-Host "Topic branch: $TOPIC_BRANCH"
Write-Host "PR_NUMBER=$PR_NUMBER"
Write-Host "TOPIC_BRANCH=$TOPIC_BRANCH"

result.detailedContent:
Skill loaded successfully ✅

# Skill: Approve Workflows and Wait for Completion

## Purpose

Approve all pending workflow runs (`action_required` status) on a PR's topic branch and wait for them to complete. This is a reusable sub-skill invoked by other shepherd skills whenever workflow approval is needed.

## Inputs

- `REPO`: Repository in `OWNER/REPO` format (e.g., `github/copilot-sdk`).
- `JTBDTASK_BRANCH`: The topic branch name associated with the PR (used to find workflow runs).
- `PR_NUMBER`: The PR number (used for `gh pr checks --watch`).

## Prerequisites

- `gh` CLI authenticated with sufficient permissions (actions, PRs).
- The PR exists and has workflow runs triggered on `JTBDTASK_BRANCH`.

---

## Procedure

### Step 1: Approve pending workflow runs

For each run in `action_required` status on the PR's branch, re-run it. The correct mechanism is `gh run rerun` (the `POST .../actions/runs/{id}/approve` endpoint is for fork PRs only and will return HTTP 403 here).

```bash
# Get all action_required runs for the PR branch
PENDING_RUNS=$(gh run list -R $REPO --branch "$JTBDTASK_BRANCH" \
  --json databaseId,conclusion --jq '.[] | select(.conclusion == "action_required") | .databaseId')

for RUN_ID in $PENDING_RUNS; do
  gh run rerun $RUN_ID -R $REPO
done
```

### Step 2: Wait for workflow runs to complete

```bash
# Watch all runs on the branch until they complete
# Use gh pr checks with --watch for convenience
gh pr checks $PR_NUMBER -R $REPO --watch --fail-fast
```

Alternatively, poll with:

```bash
gh run list -R $REPO --branch "$JTBDTASK_BRANCH" \
  --json databaseId,status,conclusion,name \
  --jq '.[] | select(.status != "completed")'
```

---

## Error handling

- **No pending runs found**: This is not an error — it means runs were already approved (possibly manually). Proceed directly to waiting for completion.
- **`gh run rerun` fails**: Retry up to 3 times with 10-second backoff, then report and stop.
- **Runs do not complete within a reasonable time**: The `--watch` flag on `gh pr checks` will block until completion or failure. If it times out, report and stop.

## Notes

- This skill is extracted from Steps 4 and 5 of `shepherd-task-from-assignment-to-ready` for reuse across multiple shepherd skills.
- The `gh api .../actions/runs/{id}/approve` endpoint does NOT work for same-repo PRs (returns HTTP 403 "This run is not from a fork pull request"). Always use `gh run rerun` instead.

arguments.command:
$REPO = "edburns/Build26-BRK206-your-agent-anywhere-multiclient-multidevice-with-github-copilot-sdk"
$JTBDTASK_BRANCH = "copilot/dd-3017826-java-real-estate-demo-e2e-testing"
$PR_NUMBER = "23"

Write-Host "=== Waiting for Copilot cloud agent to finish ==="
$TIMEOUT = 900
$INTERVAL = 30
$ELAPSED = 0

while ($ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT) {
    $STATUS = gh run list -R $REPO --branch "$JTBDTASK_BRANCH" --json databaseId,status,conclusion,name `
        --jq '.[] | select(.name == "Running Copilot cloud agent") | .status' | Select-Object -First 1
    
    Write-Host "[$ELAPSED s] Copilot agent status: $STATUS"
    
    if ($STATUS -eq "completed") {
        Write-Host "Copilot cloud agent completed!"
        break
    }
    
    Start-Sleep -Seconds $INTERVAL
    $ELAPSED += $INTERVAL
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=== All runs on branch ==="
gh run list -R $REPO --branch "$JTBDTASK_BRANCH" --json databaseId,status,conclusion,name --jq '.[] | "\(.name) | status=\(.status) | conclusion=\(.conclusion) | id=\(.databaseId)"'

Issue 3.8 requires verifying the full demo end-to-end and documenting which of the 9 Copilot SDK for Java features are demonstrated and where.

## New: `FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md`

Maps all 9 SDK features to exact class/method locations, with rationale for the one conditional feature (`web_fetch` — requires live CLI). Includes:
- 4 test scenarios (valid enquiry, spam rejection, no-match, concurrent submissions) with step-by-step phase transition tables
- Error handling validation guide (CLI unavailable, rapid submissions)
- Live-CLI and replay-proxy testing strategies
- Acceptance checklist against Phase 3.8 gating criteria

## New: Unit tests (no CLI / no container required)

**`PhaseTest`** — 9 tests covering the Phase state machine invariants:
- Exactly 3 terminal phases, 2 rejected phases
- `DONE` is terminal but not rejected
- All phases have non-blank labels

**`AgentTest`** — 18 tests covering `Agent` lifecycle:
- Full happy-path transitions: `QUEUED → VALIDATING → SEARCHING → WRITING_REPORT → DONE`
- Rejection paths: `REJECTED_GARBAGE`, `REJECTED_NO_MATCHES`
- Unknown phase name returns error without mutating state
- `finishedAt` set on terminal transition
- Event log grows correctly and returns defensive copies
- 20 concurrent virtual threads hammering `setCurrentPhase` — no corruption, no exceptions

```java
// UiUpdateSocket stubbed via anonymous subclass — no Faces runtime needed
private static final UiUpdateSocket NO_OP_SOCKET = new UiUpdateSocket() {
    @Override public void pushPhaseChange(String agentId) { }
    @Override public void pushDetailUpdate(String agentId) { }
    @Override public void pushAgentRemoved(String agentId) { }
};
```

## `pom.xml`
- JUnit Jupiter 5.11.4 (`test` scope)
- Maven Surefire 3.5.2

Issue 3.8 requires verifying the full demo end-to-end and documenting which of the 9 Copilot SDK for Java features are demonstrated and where.

## New: `FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md`

Maps all 9 SDK features to exact class/method locations, with rationale for the one conditional feature (`web_fetch` — requires live CLI). Includes:
- 4 test scenarios (valid enquiry, spam rejection, no-match, concurrent submissions) with step-by-step phase transition tables
- Error handling validation guide (CLI unavailable, rapid submissions)
- Live-CLI and replay-proxy testing strategies
- Acceptance checklist against Phase 3.8 gating criteria

## New: Unit tests (no CLI / no container required)

**`PhaseTest`** — 9 tests covering the Phase state machine invariants:
- Exactly 3 terminal phases, 2 rejected phases
- `DONE` is terminal but not rejected
- All phases have non-blank labels

**`AgentTest`** — 18 tests covering `Agent` lifecycle:
- Full happy-path transitions: `QUEUED → VALIDATING → SEARCHING → WRITING_REPORT → DONE`
- Rejection paths: `REJECTED_GARBAGE`, `REJECTED_NO_MATCHES`

// UiUpdateSocket stubbed via anonymous subclass — no Faces runtime needed

=== Full PR body ===
Issue 3.8 requires verifying the full demo end-to-end and documenting which of the 9 Copilot SDK for Java features are demonstrated and where.

## New: `FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md`

Maps all 9 SDK features to exact class/method locations, with rationale for the one conditional feature (`web_fetch` — requires live CLI). Includes:
- 4 test scenarios (valid enquiry, spam rejection, no-match, concurrent submissions) with step-by-step phase transition tables
- Error handling validation guide (CLI unavailable, rapid submissions)
- Live-CLI and replay-proxy testing strategies
- Acceptance checklist against Phase 3.8 gating criteria

## New: Unit tests (no CLI / no container required)

**`PhaseTest`** — 9 tests covering the Phase state machine invariants:
- Exactly 3 terminal phases, 2 rejected phases
- `DONE` is terminal but not rejected
- All phases have non-blank labels

**`AgentTest`** — 18 tests covering `Agent` lifecycle:
- Full happy-path transitions: `QUEUED → VALIDATING → SEARCHING → WRITING_REPORT → DONE`
- Rejection paths: `REJECTED_GARBAGE`, `REJECTED_NO_MATCHES`
- Unknown phase name returns error without mutating state
- `finishedAt` set on terminal transition
- Event log grows correctly and returns defensive copies
- 20 concurrent virtual threads hammering `setCurrentPhase` — no corruption, no exceptions

```java
// UiUpdateSocket stubbed via anonymous subclass — no Faces runtime needed
private static final UiUpdateSocket NO_OP_SOCKET = new UiUpdateSocket() {
    @Override public void pushPhaseChange(String agentId) { }
    @Override public void pushDetailUpdate(String agentId) { }
    @Override public void pushAgentRemoved(String agentId) { }
};
```

data.content: The compilation errors are in main source files. Let me check if these are pre-existing (on the base branch) or caused by Copilot's changes:

data.reasoningText:
The tests fail because the Copilot SDK for Java classes can't be found. These are compile errors for the main source (not the tests). The issue is that the main source files reference `CopilotClientOptions`, `CopilotTool`, `CopilotToolParam`, `SystemMessageConfig`, etc. which are in the `copilot-sdk-java` dependency.

This is a compilation failure of the main source code, not the tests. The tests themselves (PhaseTest and AgentTest) might reference main source classes. So the issue is that the entire project fails to compile because the SDK dependency is missing or has the wrong API.
The snapshot version of the SDK might be installed locally, but the main source code is referencing classes or APIs that don't exist in this particular snapshot version. I should check the pom.xml to see what version is specified and compare it against what classes the main source is actually trying to use.

Let me look at the git history to understand what was in the base branch and what changes were introduced.

arguments.command: git -C C:\Users\edburns\workareas\BRK206-00 --no-pager log --oneline origin/edburns/2-build-out-demo..HEAD
arguments.description: Show commits added by Copilot's PR branch

data.content:

I need to decide whether to fix the imports in the main source files or request changes from Copilot, though without CI running there's no automated way to validate the fix.

Looking at the specific import errors, it seems like there's a mismatch between what the code expects and what the SDK actually provides—the imports reference classes that don't exist at those paths in the JAR.

This is a pre-existing SDK version issue that's blocking the test compilation, separate from what Copilot was originally tasked to do. Let me examine the full JAR contents to see what classes are actually available and determine whether this is a pre-existing issue or something introduced by the recent commits.

Looking at the git history, Copilot's changes only touched FEATURE-VERIFICATION.md, pom.xml, and test files—not Agent.java or AppState.java where these errors are occurring. So these compilation failures existed before this PR. Now I need to decide whether to have Copilot fix them as part of this work or proceed with the understanding that the tests won't compile.

Since the goal is to get this to "ready for review," I should probably ask Copilot to fix those import issues directly rather than leaving a broken build. Let me also check what SystemMessageSections and SectionOverride actually are in the JAR to understand the API better.

data.reasoningText:
Now I can see the correct package paths. The main source code imports are using wrong paths. Here's the mapping:

Wrong → Correct:
1. `com.github.copilot.PermissionHandler` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.PermissionHandler`
2. `com.github.copilot.SectionOverride` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.SectionOverride`
3. `com.github.copilot.SectionOverrideAction` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.SectionOverrideAction`
4. `com.github.copilot.SessionConfig` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.SessionConfig`
5. `com.github.copilot.SystemMessageConfig` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.SystemMessageConfig`
6. `com.github.copilot.SystemMessageSection` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.SystemMessageSections` (note: Sections not Section)
7. `com.github.copilot.tool.ToolDefinition` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.ToolDefinition`
8. `com.github.copilot.tool.annotation.CopilotTool` → `com.github.copilot.tool.CopilotTool`
9. `com.github.copilot.tool.annotation.CopilotToolParam` → `com.github.copilot.tool.CopilotToolParam`
10. `com.github.copilot.CopilotClientMode` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.CopilotClientMode`
11. `com.github.copilot.CopilotClientOptions` → `com.github.copilot.rpc.CopilotClientOptions`

The `Param` class is already correctly placed in `com.github.copilot.tool`, and I'm noticing that `SystemMessageSection` becomes `SystemMessageSections` (plural) when moved to the rpc package. These appear to be pre-existing import errors.

These incorrect imports are in `Agent.java` and `AppState.java` from earlier work that's already been merged. Since the tests need to run without CLI or containers, I need the main source to compile first. I should request that Copilot fix these import paths through the PR, but let me examine `Agent.java` more closely to see if there are API usage issues beyond just the imports.
